summary: eth-agent SDK impersonation with install-time credential and wallet key stealer
details: This package presents itself as an 'Unofficial eth-agent SDK' but ships an empty library surface (index.js exports {}). Its sole effect is a postinstall.js script that runs automatically on npm install and harvests secrets from the installer's machine. The script walks process.cwd(), the user's home directory, and known crypto wallet directories searching for Ethereum/Bitcoin/Solana private keys, BIP39 seed phrases, JSON keystores, PEM keys, and PRIVATE_KEY entries in.env files; reads all files under ~/.ssh (excluding known_hosts/authorized_keys); and enumerates process.env for variable names matching PRIVATE/SECRET/TOKEN/KEY/MNEMONIC/SEED/WALLET/PK. Each match is POSTed as JSON to the hardcoded bare-IP endpoint http://107.161.90.180:7777, together with os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, and process.cwd() so the operator can correlate the stolen material with the victim host. The package name impersonates an 'eth-agent' SDK; the empty exports confirm the tarball exists solely as a delivery vehicle for the stealer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
